channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Cuba readies for first Russian oil shipment of the year as energy crisis deepens
item.description: Cuba is preparing to receive its first shipment of Russian oil this year, just days after the government announced it was operating on natural gas, solar power and thermoelectric plants as severe power outages continue to hit it.
item.content:encoded:
Cuba is preparing to receive its first shipment of Russian oil this year, just days after the government announced it was operating on natural gas, solar power and thermoelectric plants as severe power outages continue to hit it.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Reports say President Trump has damaged democracy at remarkable speed
item.description: Two leading democracy watchdog organizations are releasing studies this week that evaluate the state of American democracy after President Trump's first year.
item.content:encoded: Two leading democracy watchdog organizations are releasing studies this week that evaluate the state of American democracy after President Trump's first year.
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Trump's mediators offer Hamas formal proposal to give up its weapons in Gaza
item.description: NPR has learned that mediators have quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.
item.content:encoded:
NPR has learned that mediators have quietly given Hamas a proposal to hand over all its weapons to ensure Gaza's reconstruction.

(Image credit: Ahmad Salem)
item.dc:creator: Abu Bakr Bashir

item.title: Fear, defiance, and anger: Iranians describe life under bombardment
item.description: In messages to NPR, Tehran residents describe largely deserted streets roamed by paramilitary officials and vigilantes. They say security forces are banning gatherings for Nowruz, the Persian new year, this week.
item.content:encoded:
In messages to NPR, Tehran residents describe largely deserted streets roamed by paramilitary officials and vigilantes. They say security forces are banning gatherings for Nowruz, the Persian new year, this week.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: As Pakistan and Afghanistan declare truce, civilians in Kabul count the cost of war
item.description: At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.
item.content:encoded:
At the Emergency Hospital, dozens crowded around a thick book to check the names of the victims killed in an airstrike on a rehabilitation center. The U.N. says over a hundred people were killed.

(Image credit: Fazelminallah Qazizai for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Fazelminallah Qazizai

item.title: Israel and Iran attack gas facilities. And, Cesar Chavez accused of sexual abuse, rape
item.description: Attacks on gas facilities by Israel and Iran have escalated the war and impacted global markets. And, renowned union leader and labor rights advocate Cesar Chavez is accused of sexual abuse and rape.
item.content:encoded:
Attacks on gas facilities by Israel and Iran have escalated the war and impacted global markets. And, renowned union leader and labor rights advocate Cesar Chavez is accused of sexual abuse and rape.

(Image credit: Oliver Contreras)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran attacks world's largest liquified natural gas complex in Qatar
item.description: Iran struck Qatar's massive gas facility after Israel targeted Iran's gas field, putting global energy supplies at risk.
item.content:encoded: Iran struck Qatar's massive gas facility after Israel targeted Iran's gas field, putting global energy supplies at risk.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: What did President Trump know about Iran before going to war
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Nate Swanson, former director for Iran at the National Security Council, about what President Trump understood about Iran before going to war.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with Nate Swanson, former director for Iran at the National Security Council, about what President Trump understood about Iran before going to war.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Japan's prime minister visits the White House under shadow of Iran war
item.description: Japan Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will be the first U.S. ally to visit the White House since President Trump asked for help in sending ships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Japan Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi will be the first U.S. ally to visit the White House since President Trump asked for help in sending ships to patrol the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Tomohiro Ohsumi/Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: Why Trump's attacks and threats to Iran's Kharg Island are a big deal
item.description: If the U.S. follows through on its threats to bomb the oil assets on Kharg Island, the war would likely escalate and oil prices would increase exponentially.
item.content:encoded:
If the U.S. follows through on its threats to bomb the oil assets on Kharg Island, the war would likely escalate and oil prices would increase exponentially.

(Image credit: Atta Kenare)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller
